title: Intel 8255 Programmable Peripheral Interface (PPI)
examTip: 8255 has three 8-bit ports: A, B, and C. Port C is split into Upper (PC4-PC7) and Lower (PC0-PC3). Group A Control configures Port A and Port C Upper. Group B Control configures Port B and Port C Lower. Address pins A1, A0 select Port A (00), Port B (01), Port C (10), or Control Register (11).
theory:
The Intel 8255 is a general-purpose Programmable Peripheral Interface (PPI) chip designed to facilitate interfacing parallel I/O devices to a microprocessor. It has 24 I/O pins, which can be individually programmed in different modes of operation.

Structure and Ports:
- Port A: 8-bit input/output port with latching and buffering.
- Port B: 8-bit input/output port with latching and buffering.
- Port C: 8-bit port which can be split into two 4-bit ports: Port C Upper (PC4-PC7) and Port C Lower (PC0-PC3). It is primarily used for status handshaking signals for Ports A and B.

Control Groups:
The 24 lines are divided into two groups of 12 lines each:
- Group A Control: Configures Port A and the upper 4 bits of Port C.
- Group B Control: Configures Port B and the lower 4 bits of Port C.

Read/Write Control Logic:
Manages the data transfer. It has control pins:
- CS̄ (Chip Select): Activates the 8255 when low.
- RD̄ (Read): Enables CPU to read data/status from 8255.
- WR̄ (Write): Enables CPU to write data/control words to 8255.
- A1, A0: Address inputs that select Port A (00), Port B (01), Port C (10), or the Control Register (11) to write the Control Word.
- RESET: A high on this pin clears the control register and sets all ports to input mode.

Modes of Operation:
- Mode 0 (Basic I/O): Standard read/write with no handshaking.
- Mode 1 (Strobed I/O): Handshake signals are used via Port C to transfer data.
- Mode 2 (Strobed Bidirectional I/O): Port A is bidirectional using Port C lines for handshaking. Port B can be in Mode 0 or 1.
keyPoints: 24 I/O pins divided into three 8-bit ports: A, B, and C (split into Upper & Lower)
Group A Control manages Port A & Port C Upper; Group B Control manages Port B & Port C Lower
A1, A0 select internal registers: Port A (00), Port B (01), Port C (10), Control Reg (11)
Three major modes: Mode 0 (Basic I/O), Mode 1 (Strobed I/O), and Mode 2 (Bidirectional)
Bit Set/Reset (BSR) Mode allows individual bits of Port C to be set or cleared
useCases: Interfacing LED arrays, 7-segment displays, and keyboards to microprocessors
Handshake communication with parallel printers or slow peripheral hardware
Industrial control lines and sensor data collection in legacy embedded controllers
